Tracks Contact

Event Info

MC Hackathon 2026

RaptorHacks is Montgomery College's premier hackathon, hosted by the AI Club and Raptor Who Code. Open exclusively to current MC students, teams of 2–4 will build and present innovative projects across 7 tracks. The in-person Demo Day takes place April 11, 2026 at the Germantown campus. Each team can submit their MVP under up to 3 tracks.

With prizes, free lunch, swag bags from igniteHub, and judging by industry professionals, this is your chance to hack, compete, and showcase your skills. The GenAI track features both a virtual submission (MVP + 5-min demo video by March 22) and an in-person component on Demo Day. All teams must be present to be judged, except the AI virtual track.

Build. Compete. Win.

[ Hack the Planet ]

__ April 11, 2026

Upcoming Events

Upcoming

Networking & Team Building

March 5, 2026

Room 312, Humanities Building — Rockville Campus

Open to all registered participants

Connect with fellow hackers, find teammates, and prepare for RaptorHacks 2026. Whether you already have a team or are looking for one, this is your chance to network and strategize before the hackathon.

FAQ

Who can participate?

Only current Montgomery College students are eligible.

Is there a beginner track?

No. All tracks are open to all skill levels, but there is no dedicated beginner track.

Do I need a team?

Yes. Teams of 2–4 members are required. Find teammates on the Discord #team-formation channel.

How does the GenAI track work?

It's split into virtual and in-person. Virtual submissions (MVP + 5-min demo video) are due March 22. The top 3 present at the AI Faculty Showcase. The in-person component is due with other tracks on April 11.

When is the hackathon?

March 3 – April 11, 2026. Demo Day is April 11 at the GT campus in Germantown.

How many tracks can I submit to?

Each team can submit under up to 3 tracks.
